holdings.note: SpaceX — IPO'd Jun 2026; FMP may not yet cover earnings/dividend dates for a brand-new listing.

macro.label: Triple witching
macro.detail: Quarterly simultaneous expiry of index futures, index options & single-stock options — volume/volatility spike and pinning risk in the most options-heavy names.

INTC.read: INTC is up 12.6% in five sessions with no dated company catalyst on the board — no confirmed earnings, no ex-div in the window — so this run is sentiment/flow, not event-driven. With the position extended into a vacuum and the only marker being Sep 18 triple witching 85 days out, there's no clean add window here and no binary to trim into either; the right move is to let the tape define itself before committing either way.
INTC.bull: Five-day momentum suggests a re-rating is underway and pullbacks have been shallow.
INTC.bear: No dated catalyst to anchor the move means it can unwind just as fast on flow alone.

NVDA.read: No company-specific dated catalyst on the board for NVDA right now — next earnings isn't in the feed, so there's no binary print to trade around. The 8-share position is down ~4% on the week at $198.94, which is a normal pullback in an uptrending megacap rather than a thesis break. With only a distant triple witching as a calendar marker, the right posture is to let the next earnings date get confirmed before pre-positioning either way.
NVDA.next_catalyst: 2026-09-18 — Triple witching (macro, no NVDA-specific catalyst in feed)
NVDA.bull: AI capex cycle still intact; a ~4% dip on no news is the kind of weakness that historically gets bought ahead of the next print.
NVDA.bear: Without a dated earnings catalyst to anchor to, further multiple compression or hyperscaler capex wobble could extend the drawdown unchecked.

ORCL.read: ORCL is in a real drawdown — down 4.6% today and 16% over five sessions — but you've given me no dated company catalyst to anchor a window. Without an earnings date or ex-div on the board, this is a tape read, not an event read: the next true catalyst is the next print, and until that's on the calendar there's no defined add zone. Triple witching on 2026-09-18 is months out and not ORCL-specific, so it doesn't drive the decision here.
ORCL.next_catalyst: 2026-09-18 — Triple witching (macro only; no ORCL-specific dated catalyst provided)
ORCL.bull: AI/cloud capex narrative intact; a -16% week resets sentiment and sets up a sharper bounce into the next print.
ORCL.bear: No dated catalyst to stop the bleed; momentum break of this size often retests lower before a real base forms.

NOK.read: No dated NOK-specific catalysts on the board — next earnings isn't even on the calendar yet, and the only macro marker is triple witching 85 days out, which is noise for a $13.80 telecom equipment name. Tape is flat (+0.7% on the day, -1.2% on the week), so there's no stretched winner to trim and no capitulation to add into. Sit on the 174 shares until a dated print, dividend declaration, or AI/networking-deal headline gives you an actual window.
NOK.next_catalyst: 2026-09-18 — Triple witching (macro only; no NOK-specific dated catalyst on file)
NOK.bull: Networking/AI infra narrative plus a confirmed earnings date or contract headline would re-rate the name off this base.
NOK.bear: Without a near-term catalyst, the position drifts with the tape and capital sits idle in a low-beta name.

NBIS.read: No dated company catalysts on the board for NBIS and only a tiny 1-share position, so there's no real add/trim window to play here. The only thing ahead is triple witching on 2026-09-18, which is a tape event, not an NBIS-specific driver. Until an earnings date or a capacity/customer announcement gets posted, this is a watch — let the next dated catalyst define the setup.
NBIS.bull: AI infra demand and Nvidia-aligned GPU cloud story keeps NBIS a momentum vehicle into any dated print.
NBIS.bear: Without a dated catalyst, the name trades on sentiment and beta — vulnerable to any AI-capex wobble or options-driven flush around witching.

KO.read: Tiny 5-share KO position with no dated catalyst on the board — no earnings or ex-div in the queue, and price is flat (+0.4% on the day and week) around $80.62. Without a print or dividend date to trade against, there's no defined window here; the only thing on the calendar is triple witching 85 days out, which is a tape event, not a KO-specific catalyst. Wait for the next earnings or ex-div date to populate before framing an add/trim plan.
KO.bull: Defensive staples compounder drifting quietly; flat tape preserves optionality until a dated catalyst appears.
KO.bear: No near-term catalyst means no edge — capital sits idle in a low-beta name while other setups have dated windows.

SPCX.read: SPCX is a freshly-public SpaceX listing (June 2026 IPO) with no confirmed earnings or dividend dates on the tape yet — data providers haven't picked up the cycle, so there's no dated company catalyst to anchor a window. With only 10 shares, this is a small starter; the real question is whether the post-IPO lockup/quiet-period unlock and the first earnings print show up on the calendar before you sit through Sept triple witching, which can amplify volatility in low-float new issues. Until a dated event prints, treat this as a price-action name rather than an event name.
SPCX.next_catalyst: 2026-09-18 — Triple witching (only dated event on the calendar; no company catalysts confirmed yet)
SPCX.bull: Scarcity asset, brand-new SpaceX listing — first earnings beat and any unlock-related supply absorption could re-rate it.
SPCX.bear: No confirmed earnings/dividend dates, post-IPO lockup overhang risk, and triple-witching can whipsaw thin new-issue floats.

disclaimer: Forward earnings dates are third-party aggregator estimates, not company-confirmed — confirm against the company's IR release (~2 weeks ahead). Dividends, macro prints and witching are scheduled/confirmed. Not investment advice.
